channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19157] Ban clients from nodemap
item.description:
Administrators might want to immediately prevent access to Lustre for a particular client, based on criteria such as client activity or generated load on the file system.

In addition to the ability for administrators to shut off this client, it would also be useful to be able to deny this client access to data administratively. This would provide a stronger ability to protect access to Lustre, especially in cloud environments where Lustre admins may not have the ability to interact with or shut off client nodes.

In order to address this requirement, we could add a new 'banlist' nodemap property. So once a storage admin has identified clients from a given nodemap that must be blocked, they are able to add theirs NIDs to this ban list. The banned clients would still be considered part of that nodemap, but all file system accesses from those clients would be blocked. This means not only new mounts from the banned clients would be blocked, but also existing mounts would not be permitted to access Lustre anymore. Later on, as the clients are removed from the ban list, they would be able to mount Lustre again, and get back access to Lustre.

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:

Project: fs/lustre-release

Current Patch Set:
Commit: 64a0a6c4559b2951ee78fca0a32fa97c3d2d4fde

"Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61755
Subject: LU-19157 nodemap: support banlist with gss identification
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: a704efbe3c8ae17cbe31ca09e2774be66e11a045

Sebastien, it looks like sanity-sec test_21 was failing on 2025-09-17 with patch https://review.whamcloud.com/61346 ("LU-19157 nodemap: revoke export locks on nodemap change") before it landed, and now this subtest started failing on b_es6_0 with the last batch of patch landings, so it looks like there was some regression in one of the patches that landed:

60851502fd RM-620 build: New tag 2.14.0-ddn226
45729ef2ec LU-19139 lnet: add accept_port_bulk module parameter
3a88c9c18b EX-12327 krb: use sha256 nodemap name from principal
5b1187619b LU-9648 ptlrpc: Add kernel doc style for ptlrpc (21)
13ac307e45 LU-19253 target: get all NIDs at start in one call
5bcd5cb536 LU-19411 changelog: fix wrong GC triggering
51ffd069e1 LU-19399 utils: Fix NULL check in copy_file_expandable()
62336dc595 LU-8066 obdclass: Use spinlock when accessing export nodemap
a8e64ab6b0 LU-19157 nodemap: revoke export locks on nodemap change
2bccacc912 LU-19229 lnet: change LNetAddPeer() error handling
a40cc9acb4 LU-19389 mgc: check reply in mgc_nid_notify_interpret()
30d2a86587 LU-19268 lmv: Add qos_exclude_patterns
4b3b237770 EX-12849 tests: improve sanity-compr test_2030
9c43a5921b LU-14669 tests: reduce time spent in sanity-sec
1d979be771 (tag: 2.14.0-ddn225) RM-620 build: New tag 2.14.0-ddn225

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60984/
Subject: LU-19157 nodemap: read semaphore for ban_range_search

Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 5a3722af0849e6503777f46b163a5372fde3e6d9

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60951/
Subject: LU-19157 nodemap: fix sanity-sec test_81b
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: ccbddde1867b466a87a1a3d7251fb2ccd4f8248a

"Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61090
Subject: LU-19157 nodemap: make nodemap name optional for banlist
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: c5702e4d1c005d4babac7e53d4be667e57331f94

As banlists use the same mechanism as regular NID ranges, I guess what you are saying would apply to both?
By "range overlap in the same nodemap should just be handled by the tools", do you mean the tools should automatically adapt the range to be added, so that it fits along the already existing ones?
For instance, let's consider nodemap nm0 has a banlist of [10.10.1.100, 10.10.1.200]. If we want to add [10.10.1.150, 10.10.1.255] to nm0 banlist, you mean the tools should see the overlap, and only add [10.10.1.201, 10.10.1.255]?
I think range overlap in the same nodemap should just be handled by the tools. Range overlap in different nodemaps should be an error.

"Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60984
Subject: LU-19157 nodemap: read semaphore for ban_range_search
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 7b615a60486f8c100f0c65de0f7092e7a97cb492

"Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60951
Subject: LU-19157 nodemap: fix sanity-sec test_81b
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: c1713502a3e56e89b622193e26f4726fce0dfe2c

-EEXIST is also returned in case of range overlap, which is valuable. In the case of sanity-sec test_81b, the problem comes from the fact that the test assumes $HOSTNAME is $clients_arr[0], and then tries to add the same NID twice. Instead of relying on setup_local_client_nodemap() to setup the nodemap, it should do it directly to have full control.

It appears that the newly-added sanity-sec test_81b is failing intermittently now that it is landed (and possibly even before it was landed):
https://testing.whamcloud.com/search?horizon=15552000&status%5B%5D=FAIL&test_set_script_id=5984404e-4b34-11e0-a7f6-52540025f9af&sub_test_script_id=e04460e0-7a68-4c3a-b5f3-cee67c6816c7&source=sub_tests#redirect

CMD: trevis-57vm7 /usr/sbin/lctl nodemap_add c0
CMD: trevis-57vm7 /usr/sbin/lctl nodemap_add_range --name c0 --range 10.240.40.231@tcp
CMD: trevis-57vm7 /usr/sbin/lctl nodemap_modify --name c0 --property admin --value 1
CMD: trevis-57vm7 /usr/sbin/lctl nodemap_modify --name c0 --property trusted --value 1
CMD: trevis-57vm7 /usr/sbin/lctl nodemap_activate 1
:
CMD: trevis-57vm7 /usr/sbin/lctl nodemap_add_range --name c0 --range 10.240.40.231@tcp
trevis-57vm7: error: invalid ioctl: 000ce042 errno: 17: File exists
trevis-57vm7: nodemap_add_range: File exists

Is there really any benefit to return -EEXIST if adding a range that already exists? That makes sense if the NID is already in a different nodemap, but if it is in the same nodemap then it just makes life difficult for the user. That makes it difficult to run an "add these NIDs to the nodemap" script more than once without getting lots of errors (which will eventually be ignored and/or hide other errors), or adding complexity to userspace to check if that NID already exists in a nodemap, which can be easily checked (and ignored) in the kernel.

Subject: LU-19157 nodemap: introduce range type
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 785bce54e2b48a54f27c8e597697998d6992f7d2

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60026/
Subject: LU-19157 nodemap: nid range generic functions
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 71338d2791720ca0d38a59734651431840dcd75f

"Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60643
Subject: LU-19157 nodemap: revoke export locks on nodemap change
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 1f02920f6e4e21ed341d269664ef4433a60edc40

As for returning the "banned" status to the client, there is pb_flags (and possibly pb_op_flags) that could be used. pb_flags holds "PTLRPC_MSG_*" and has lots of space. While pb_op_flags applies to a specific RPC type (mostly used for *_CONNECT) I think this is generic for all RPC types (not just connect) so should go in pb_flags like

I have pushed patch "LU-19157 nodemap: forward ban status to client" at https://review.whamcloud.com/60122 to address this.

"Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60122
Subject: LU-19157 nodemap: forward ban status to client
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: c1de9d4d705bb44a9a4af4251c3141e730b989f8

"Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60095
Subject: LU-19157 dbg: debug ban list on default
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 8268afb3dcf41ec894eeeeeee9c348e14e518f34

"Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60074
Subject: LU-19157 nodemap: allow ban list on default nodemap
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 74f3e3d865d3c78a05a56874c10be0c39372179e

I would suggest that "redeemed" (while a good descriptive word) is not closely associated to "banned", and an unfamiliar reader may not associate those terms together. While not proper English, "un-banned" would be easier to understand?

Also, CWARN() has the traditional "debugging" output (timestamps, function name, filename, line number), but since this is more of an administrative/expected result, then LCONSOLE_WARN() might be more appropriate/pretty?

As for returning the "banned" status to the client, there is pb_flags (and possibly pb_op_flags) that could be used. pb_flags holds "PTLRPC_MSG_*" and has lots of space. While pb_op_flags applies to a specific RPC type (mostly used for *_CONNECT) I think this is generic for all RPC types (not just connect) so should go in pb_flags like:

#define PTLRPC_MSG_CLIENT_BANNED 0x0100

Obviously we don't want the client to print an error continuously when it is banned, but it might make sense to print something periodically (e.g. every 6h) if so that this appears in the logs if someone is wondering why their client doesn't work. For old clients that don't understand the PTLRPC_MSG_CLIENT_BANNED they would just get -EPERM errors and not print anything, but this would be printed on the server console.

I refreshed patch https://review.whamcloud.com/60029, here are the messages printed in the server logs.

  • When a client is added to the ban list of a nodemap: 
    00000001:08000000:0.0F:1751874123.541227:0:91582:0:(nodemap_member.c:188:nm_member_reclassify_nodemap()) seb-MDT0000: nodemap nm0 banning client 7beca187-1608-4aa3-be98-a07972a0097c (at 192.168.66.206@tcp)
  • Then when the client tries to access the file system: 
    00000020:00080000:1.0:1751874550.853517:0:91625:0:(tgt_handler.c:802:tgt_request_handle()) operation 37 from peer 12345-192.168.66.206@tcp banned by nodemap nm0: rc=-1
  • When a banned client tries to mount: 
    00000001:00000400:0.0:1751875595.380176:0:91819:0:(nodemap_handler.c:589:nodemap_add_member()) seb-MDT0000: adding banned NID 192.168.66.206@tcp to nodemap nm0
00000020:00080000:0.0:1751875595.380819:0:91819:0:(tgt_handler.c:802:tgt_request_handle()) operation 41 from peer 12345-192.168.66.206@tcp banned by nodemap nm0: rc=-1
  • When a client is removed from the ban list: 
    00000001:08000000:0.0:1751875442.582976:0:91582:0:(nodemap_member.c:195:nm_member_reclassify_nodemap()) seb-MDT0000: nodemap nm0 redeemed client 7beca187-1608-4aa3-be98-a07972a0097c (at 192.168.66.206@tcp)

I also added the banned info to the exports list associated with nodemaps:

MDS# lctl get_param nodemap.nm0.exports
nodemap.nm0.exports=
[
 { nid: 192.168.66.206@tcp, uuid: 90471ae9-0f80-4e11-98d0-143862a6ad43, dev: seb-MDT0000, banned: true }
]

I will have a look at how the banned status can be put in the reply to the client, so that it can print a useful message.

One thing I didn't see in the patches is a printing the "banned" status in the client export on the server, or some way to see on the client import (and syslog) that it has been banned.

Since this is likely an uncommon event, it probably makes sense to print a CWARN() in the client and server syslog with the client NID, nodemap name (server). We could return a flag in the ptlrpc_body indicating that the client export has been banned, so that it can print the banned status on the client. It would be useful to return some actual reason for the ban, possibly in the jobid field (up to 32 chars) since that is only used for client->server RPCs and not server->client RPCs.
It would be desirable (if possible) to allow blocking clients from the default nodemap, in addition to a specific nodemap.
